rulings.Case Title: CP III RINCON TOWERS, INC., A COLORADO CORPORATION VS. ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY ANAD COUNTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Amended Petition For Writ On Mandate And/Or Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER CP III RINCON TOWERS, INC.'s Amended Petition For Writ On Mandate And/Or Writ Of Administrative Mandate. Further hearing required. The Court finds no prejudice to the Assessment Appeals Board ("the Board")- not the Real Party in Interest - in hearing argument by Petitioner as well as Real Party in Interest in a refund hearing. Real Party in Interest submits that the refunds are not issued by the Board but by the Tax Collector or County Auditor, neither of whom are parties to this action, and that refunds can only be issued to the party that paid the tax, thus preventing deposit of the refund, if any, into an escrow account pending settlement or order of the Court. Petitioner should address these issues and provide the Court with direction as to how, if at all, a refund can be interpleaded with the Court pending settlement or court order directing disbursement. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WONG et al VS. THE OLYMPIC CLUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, GREG DEROSA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Granted. As an initial matter, Plaintiffs' response to the separate statement does not comply with CCP sec. 437c(b)(3) and CRC 3.1350(h). Although this is a ground for granting the motion, the Court also considered the merits of the motion, as discussed below. As to the fourth cause of action for discrimination as to Mr. Wong, Defendants met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Mr. Wong suffered an adverse employment action because of his age or ethnicity. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 3, 66-67.) Plaintiffs have not raised triable issues of material fact. As to the fifth cause of action for retaliation as to Mr. Wong, Defendants met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Mr. Wong suffered an adverse employment action or that any action occurred because of Mr. Wong's age or ethnicity. (UMF 3, 66-67.) Plaintiffs have not raised triable issues of material fact. As to the sixth cause of action for discrimination as to Ms. Canion, Defendants have met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Ms. Canion suffered an adverse employment action because of her age or ethnicity. (UMF 107, 132, 137.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact. As to the seventh cause of action for retaliation as to Ms. Canion, Defendants met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Ms. Canion suffered an adverse employment action or that any action occurred because of Ms. Canion's age or ethnicity. (UMF 107, 125, 132, 137.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact. As to the eighth cause of action for harassment against Defendant Greg DeRosa, Defendants met their initial burden by establishing that the complained of conduct was not severe or pervasive, nor was it motivated by Mr. Wong's age or race. (UMF 70-75.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IVAN CHAVEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint fails to state a cause of action under the Police Officer's Bill of Rights (Govt. Code § 3300, et seq.) because Plaintiff must first petition the court for a writ of administrative mandate following the Defendants' decision to terminate Plaintiff's employment. Gales v. Superior Court (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1596, 1602-03. The First Amended Complaint also fails to state a cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy because this cause of action is not authorized by statute. Govt. Code §§ 815(a) & 815.6; Miklosky v. Regents of the Univ. of Cal. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 898-99; Thorn v. City of Glendale (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 1379, 1384-85. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Expert Disclosure; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff's Expert Disclosure; Request For Sanctions. Granted. Plaintiff is ordered to produce an expert disclosure identifying the non-retained experts she actually intends to call to testify at trial on or before the close of business on October 31, 2014. Plaintiff's disclosure of "custodians of records" as experts is improper. Plaintiff must name the custodians of records if she intends to call them as expert witnesses. Sanctions are denied. rulings.Case Title: ELSIE DEPIO VS. HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication; Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME CARE ADVOCATES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment or adjudication is denied in its entirety. The declaration of Plaintiff Elsie Depio creates a triable issue of material fact regarding how many hours a day Plaintiff worked for defendant's client and what Plaintiff's tasks were. (Elsie Depio dec., pars. 3-9 and 10-19). The declaration shows that there are disputed factual issues regarding whether Plaintiff was paid a minimum wage and whether she is an exempt personal attendant under IWC Order 15-2001. rulings.Case Title: PATRICE RENEE MOTLEY VS. NIRAV NALIN TOLIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court discloses that defense counsel Greg Chin used to work for the court, but the court does not believe that his prior employment is a basis to recuse itself from this case. The demurrer to the complaint is overruled. Cause of action 1 sufficiently pleads a claim for willful misconduct. See Carlsen v. Koivumaki (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 879, 895. Cause of action 3 sufficiently pleads a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff's allegations regarding defendant's aggressive and dangerous driving, causing the accident, and then fleeing the scene of the accident demonstrate outrageous conduct with reckless disregard to causing emotional distress. The court rejects defendant's argument that the allegations in the amended complaint contradict the original complaint and/or are a sham. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICE RENEE MOTLEY VS. NIRAV NALIN TOLIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court grants and denies the motion to strike. The court grants the motion without leave to amend as to paragraphs 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 52 and 53. Those allegations regarding defendant's employment are irrelevant to plaintiff's claim. The court grants the motion without leave to amend as to paragraphs 14 and 15 as irrelevant. The allegation that defendant is habitually an aggressive/angry driver is irrelevant and improper character evidence. Plaintiff properly alleges that defendant was driving in an angry/aggressive manner immediately prior to the accident at paragraphs 20 and 22. The court grants the motion without leave to amend as to the phrase "longstanding, habitually aggressive and angry driving and" in paragraph 44. The court denies the motion as to the remainder of paragraph 44 and denies as to the prayer for punitive damages in paragraph c of the prayer. The court strikes paragraph 10(d) of the prayer [attorney's fees] without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to cite a statutory, legal, or contractual basis for the prayer for fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Shirley Van Bibber And Kurt Neumann To Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $9932.50 Against Shirley Van Bibber, Kurt Neumann, Niesar & Vestal Llp And John Kelley. rulings.Case Title: SBERLO FAMILY TRUST VS. TANYA PHILLIPS (3/19/12-AMENDED TO CONTRACT ACTION)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Tanya Phillips' Motion For Possession Of The Rental Property
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 14, Defendant Tanya Phillips' Motion For Possession Of The Rental Property. Denied. Restitution in the form of possession of the real property would be inequitable because the subject property has been occupied by another tenant since March 2012. Stockton Theatres, Inc. v. Palermo (1953) 121 Cal.App.2d 616, 619. rulings.Case Title: SBERLO FAMILY TRUST VS. TANYA PHILLIPS (3/19/12-AMENDED TO CONTRACT ACTION)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Tanya Phillips' Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Yoel And Nadine Sberlo And Their Counsel Karen Uchiyama
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 31, 2014, Line 14, Defendant Tanya Phillips' Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Yoel And Nadine Sberlo And Their Counsel Karen Uchiyama. Denied. The Plaintiffs'/Cross-Defendants' Anti-SLAPP Motion was not frivolous pursuant to C.C.P. § 425.16(c) because the Defendant/Cross-Complainant failed to demonstrate that the Anti-SLAPP Motion lacked merit. The Court of Appeal's reversal of the lower court's order granting the Anti-SLAPP Motion is insufficient to show that "any reasonable attorney would agree the motion was totally devoid of merit." See Gerbosi v. Gaims, Weil, West & Epstein, LLP (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 435, 450. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
